page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core processing is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is strong in both read and write operations, though small-block cached reads remain average. The hardware excels in LLM inference tasks; prompt processing for 135M and 7B models ranks in the top 10%, while text generation for these models is strong. Large 70B model prompt processing drops to average.

The g4dn.16xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring both GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage. The qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the inclusion of a single Turing T4 GPU alongside a high vCPU count and 256 GB of RAM, though the older CPU architecture presents a tradeoff in single-threaded performance. This instance is suited for machine learning inference, graphics-intensive applications, video transcoding, and database operations that leverage local NVMe storage and multi-threaded CPU capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, it features 64 dedicated vCPUs, 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and networking delivers a 50 Gbps baseline. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, multi-core performance is average, and large-block memory bandwidth is strong. The instance demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing for small and medium models. This hardware profile is suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and workloads requiring high network throughput and local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 16 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 900 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 900 GB NVMe storage for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking for machine learning inference workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 memory
Single NVIDIA Turing T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM
900 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM inference
Suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and multi-threaded workloads
